summary: Frontend-only validation of Lead Proposal share migration to the Document Sharing Framework + BCC support. All 5 requested scenarios PASS after fixing a seed-data issue (proposal had tenant_id=null which prevented /api/leads/{id}/proposal from returning the proposal; updated to nyla-air-water).

minor.issue: Existing seed proposal for lead 2ac8be7f-7115-4f40-b5ef-b03113146606 had tenant_id=null. The share resolver _resolve_lead_proposal falls back to a no-tenant query and so still worked in curl, but the tenant-scoped get_lead_proposal route returns null, hiding the Share button. Fixed in DB by setting tenant_id='nyla-air-water'. Worth investigating in seed scripts so this doesn't regress.

action_items: Audit seed/test data scripts that create lead_proposals — ensure tenant_id is always set; otherwise /api/leads/{id}/proposal returns null and the proposal Share button never renders even though the share resolver fallback can still find the doc.

Optional: tighten the resolver in /app/backend/services/share_resolvers.py _resolve_lead_proposal so it does NOT silently fall back to a no-tenant query — that would have surfaced this data issue immediately instead of masking it.
critical_code_review_comments: share_resolvers._resolve_lead_proposal contains a fallback query without tenant_id (`db.lead_proposals.find_one({"lead_id": document_id})`) which is a multi-tenant data-leak risk (in a multi-tenant install another tenant's proposal could be returned for an id collision). Remove the fallback OR scope it to a stricter context.

LeadDetail.js: the legacy bespoke proposal share dialog code still exists in the file (per the request the share-via-email button now uses <ShareButton>). Confirm the dead dialog code path is fully unreachable and consider removing it to keep file size down (LeadDetail.js is already 2410 lines).
seed_data_creation: Updated existing lead_proposals doc for lead 2ac8be7f-7115-4f40-b5ef-b03113146606: set tenant_id='nyla-air-water' (was null). Also, by exercising the admin policy save during testing, an Always-Bcc entry 'archive@nylaairwater.earth' was added to the lead_proposal policy; it now persists as expected (called out as acceptable in the request).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Lead 2ac8be7f-7115-4f40-b5ef-b03113146606 (Citadel Hotels & Conventions By Vinncca) has an APPROVED proposal test_proposal.pdf. The lead's primary contact has NO email so the share dialog's To section is empty (expected). The lead_proposal admin policy now contains archive@nylaairwater.earth in Always-Bcc — that's why the Bcc section auto-reveals with that chip when opening the proposal share dialog. surya has no reporting manager so cc_manager=true only injects the sender (Surya Yadavalli) into CC.

test1_dialog_open_prefill.subject_prefilled: Nyla Air Water - Proposal for review

test2_send.note: Success toast was no longer in DOM after 8s sleep (auto-dismissed). Dialog close on success confirms the POST /api/share succeeded.

test3_empty_to_validation.toast: Add at least one recipient in To

test4_admin_policy_screen.save_toast: Saved recipient rules for Lead Proposal
